plan.title: packs:slice v2 -- broaden rules-pack source to AGENTS.md + main.md

narratives.Description: Broaden the rules pack source coverage beyond `coding/*` to also ingest the RFC2119 directives in AGENTS.md and main.md, and capture full bodies for every `coding/*.md` rule source document (not just the single `coding/testing.md` proof). REFINED 2026-06-16: the needs_refinement design question -- how migrating AGENTS.md interacts with `task agents:refresh` and the managed-section contract -- is resolved by an explicit ownership boundary. AGENTS.md and main.md are ingested as directive METADATA ONLY (id/tier/domain/text/path with body=null) and are NEVER rendered as `packs:render` projections, so AGENTS.md stays solely owned by `task agents:refresh` and the dual-owner collision is structurally impossible: the renderer already skips null-body entries (scripts/pack_render.py documents-mode `if not body: continue`). Body capture / projection rendering broadens only within the collision-safe `coding/*` set. AGENTS.md's managed-section block (delimited by `<!-- deft:managed-section -->` ... `<!-- /deft:managed-section -->`) is stripped before directive extraction because it is a rendered mirror of templates/agents-entry.md, not a canonical source -- this prevents duplicate directive entries. The rules-pack native schema enrichment fields remain out of scope (owned by #748).
narratives.DesignNote: agents:refresh ownership boundary (resolves needs_refinement). AGENTS.md + main.md contribute directive metadata only (body=null, never rendered); `packs:render` writes only `coding/*.md` projections, so AGENTS.md remains owned solely by `task agents:refresh`. The renderer skips null-body entries (scripts/pack_render.py documents-mode), and a guard test (item a4) asserts AGENTS.md/main.md bodies stay null, so the two generators can never both write AGENTS.md. AGENTS.md's managed section is excluded from extraction (rendered mirror of templates/agents-entry.md). This boundary is what makes the story concurrency-safe and no longer needs_refinement.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Extend `scripts/pack_migrate_rules.py` to parse two additional explicit source files beyond the `coding/*.md` glob: AGENTS.md (domain `agents`) and main.md (domain `main`). Reuse the existing `parse_rules` glyph+prose extractor (AGENTS.md/main.md use the same `!` / `~` / `?` / SHOULD-NOT / MUST-NOT glyphs). For AGENTS.md, strip the `<!-- deft:managed-section -->` ... `<!-- /deft:managed-section -->` block before extraction (rendered mirror, not canonical). These two sources are metadata-only: every entry gets body=null and is therefore skipped by the renderer.
2. Broaden body capture so EVERY `coding/*.md` doc carries its full body (regenerate-and-commit projection per ADR-001), not just the coding/testing.md proof; AGENTS.md/main.md stay body=null.
3. Update `vbrief/schemas/rules-pack.schema.json`: broaden the `path` pattern from `^coding/.+\.md$` to also permit `AGENTS.md` and `main.md` (e.g. `^(coding/.+\.md|AGENTS\.md|main\.md)$`); refresh the schema + `generated_from` description to name the broadened source set. No domain enum exists (domain is a free `^[a-z0-9-]+$` slug) so `agents`/`main` need no enum edit.
4. Regenerate: `task packs:render-rules` (rebuild pack JSON), `task packs:render -- --pack rules` (write coding/* projections), then `task packs:render -- --pack rules --check` and `task packs:render -- --check` (drift-clean; record the new total projection count for the test update).
5. Add assertions to `tests/cli/test_pack_migrate_rules.py` and `tests/cli/test_pack_render.py`: (a) rules entries exist with domain `agents` and `main`; (b) every `coding/*.md` doc has a non-null body; (c) GUARD: no entry whose path is AGENTS.md or main.md ever has a non-null body; (d) update the multipack projection-count assertion. Confirm `task verify:pack-drift` is green.
narratives.UserStory: As an agent loading framework rules, I want AGENTS.md and main.md directives represented in the rules pack, so that I can slice the full rule corpus from canonical JSON instead of only the coding directives.

narrative.Acceptance: The rules pack JSON stores directive entries with domain `agents` (from AGENTS.md, managed-section excluded) and domain `main` (from main.md), in addition to the existing coding directives.

The rules pack stores a non-null body for every `coding/*.md` source document (not only coding/testing.md), each rendered as a banner-marked drift-checked projection; AGENTS.md and main.md entries stay body=null.
`task packs:slice rules by-domain --domain agents` and `--domain main` return the new directives, and `task packs:render -- --check` / `task verify:pack-drift` validate with no drift.
A guard test asserts no rules entry whose path is AGENTS.md or main.md carries a non-null body, so `packs:render` never writes AGENTS.md and the sole owner of AGENTS.md remains `task agents:refresh`.

expected_outputs: Rules pack entries with domain agents + main (AGENTS.md managed-section excluded)
Every coding/*.md body captured and rendered drift-clean
Guard test locking AGENTS.md/main.md body=null (agents:refresh ownership boundary)
swarm.refinement_note: Refined 2026-06-16: resolved the agents:refresh managed-section interaction via the metadata-only / never-rendered ownership boundary (see narratives.DesignNote). readiness needs_refinement -> ready.
swarm.missing_traces_justification: Framework-internal packs:slice v2 work traced to GitHub issue #1637; schema enrichment portion deferred to #748; no specification.vbrief.json spec-section applies.

references.title: Issue #1637: feat(packs): packs:slice v2 -- remaining lessons slices + skills/rules/strategies pack surfaces
